title_en: Importance for Students and Available Editions
title_id: Kepentingan bagi Pelajar dan Edisi yang Tersedia
content_en:
Al-Burhan is an important work for students of Quranic sciences, both for its substantive content and for its historical significance as the founding text of the unified field of ulum al-Quran. Students who are learning the Quranic sciences in depth should engage with both al-Burhan and al-Itqan, comparing their treatments of the same topics to understand both the earlier tradition and how it was developed by as-Suyuti. For many topics, especially those in the linguistic and rhetorical sciences of the Quran, az-Zarkashi's treatment is deeper and more useful than as-Suyuti's.

For students interested in the history of Quranic scholarship, al-Burhan is essential as a document of the state of the field in the fourteenth century. Comparing it with al-Itqan reveals what remained stable and what changed in the tradition over half a century, and understanding these dynamics is important for historical study of Islamic religious scholarship.

The work is accessible to students with solid Arabic and a background in Quranic studies. The language is clear and the organization systematic enough that specific topics can be located without reading the work from beginning to end. For sustained study, however, reading at least the most important chapters in sequence provides a better sense of az-Zarkashi's overall approach and the coherence of his framework.

The standard Arabic edition is the four-volume set edited by Muhammad Abu al-Fadl Ibrahim, the same editor who produced the standard edition of al-Itqan, published in Cairo. This edition has been widely reprinted and is available in both print and digital forms. Other editions, including a more recent critical edition by Dar al-Turath, are available for academic use. The complete text is in major digital Islamic libraries and can be searched and cross-referenced with al-Itqan for comparative study. Students who take the time to compare al-Burhan and al-Itqan on the same topics are rewarded with insight into how Islamic scholarship accumulates and refines itself across generations: they can trace exactly what as-Suyuti added to az-Zarkashi's treatment, where he agreed and where he diverged, and how he reorganized the material for greater accessibility. This comparative exercise is itself an education in the history of Islamic Quranic scholarship that no secondary account can adequately substitute for.
content_id:
Al-Burhan adalah karya penting bagi para pelajar ilmu-ilmu Al-Qur'an, baik karena konten substantifnya maupun karena signifikansi historisnya sebagai teks pendiri bidang ulum al-Qur'an yang terpadu. Para pelajar yang mempelajari ilmu-ilmu Al-Qur'an secara mendalam sebaiknya terlibat dengan al-Burhan maupun al-Itqan, membandingkan perlakuan keduanya atas topik-topik yang sama untuk memahami tradisi awal maupun bagaimana ia dikembangkan oleh al-Suyuthi. Untuk banyak topik, terutama yang berkaitan dengan ilmu-ilmu linguistik dan retoris Al-Qur'an, perlakuan al-Zarkasyi lebih mendalam dan lebih berguna daripada perlakuan al-Suyuthi.

Bagi para pelajar yang tertarik pada sejarah keilmuan Al-Qur'an, al-Burhan sangat diperlukan sebagai dokumen tentang keadaan bidang ini pada abad keempat belas. Membandingkannya dengan al-Itqan mengungkap apa yang tetap stabil dan apa yang berubah dalam tradisi selama setengah abad, dan memahami dinamika-dinamika ini penting untuk studi historis keilmuan agama Islam.

Karya ini dapat diakses oleh para pelajar dengan bahasa Arab yang kuat dan latar belakang dalam studi Al-Qur'an. Bahasanya jelas dan organisasinya cukup sistematis sehingga topik-topik tertentu dapat ditemukan tanpa membaca karya dari awal hingga akhir. Untuk studi yang berkelanjutan, namun demikian, membaca setidaknya bab-bab terpenting secara berurutan memberikan gambaran yang lebih baik tentang pendekatan keseluruhan al-Zarkasyi dan koherensi kerangkanya.

Edisi standar dalam bahasa Arab adalah set empat jilid yang diedit oleh Muhammad Abu al-Fadhl Ibrahim, editor yang sama yang menghasilkan edisi standar al-Itqan, diterbitkan di Kairo. Edisi ini telah banyak dicetak ulang dan tersedia dalam bentuk cetak maupun digital. Edisi-edisi lain, termasuk edisi kritis yang lebih baru oleh Dar al-Turats, tersedia untuk keperluan akademis. Teks lengkapnya ada di perpustakaan-perpustakaan digital Islam besar dan dapat dicari serta dirujuk silang dengan al-Itqan untuk studi komparatif. Para pelajar yang meluangkan waktu untuk membandingkan al-Burhan dan al-Itqan pada topik-topik yang sama mendapatkan wawasan tentang bagaimana keilmuan Islam berakumulasi dan menyempurnakan dirinya lintas generasi: mereka dapat menelusuri dengan tepat apa yang al-Suyuthi tambahkan pada perlakuan al-Zarkasyi, di mana ia setuju dan di mana ia berbeda, serta bagaimana ia mengorganisasikan ulang materi untuk aksesibilitas yang lebih besar. Latihan komparatif ini sendiri merupakan pendidikan dalam sejarah keilmuan Al-Qur'an Islam yang tidak dapat digantikan secara memadai oleh laporan sekunder apa pun.
content_ar: Al-Burhan is an المهم work for students of القرآني sciences, both for its substantive content and for its historical significance as the founding text of the unified field of ulum al-القرآن الكريم. Students who are learning the القرآني sciences in depth should engage with both al-Burhan and al-Itqan, comparing their treatments of the same topics لفهم both the earlier التراث and how it was developed by as-Suyuti. For many topics, ولا سيما those in the linguistic and rhetorical sciences of the القرآن الكريم, az-Zarkashi's treatment is deeper and more useful than as-Suyuti's. For students interested in the history of القرآني scholarship, al-Burhan is الجوهري as a document of the state of the field in the fourteenth century. Comparing it with al-Itqan reveals what remained stable and what changed in the التراث over half a century, and الفهم these dynamics is المهم for historical study of Islamic religious scholarship. The work is accessible to students with solid Arabic and a background in القرآني studies. The language is clear and the organization systematic enough that specific topics can be located without reading the work from beginning to end. For sustained study, غير أن, reading at least the most المهم الأبواب in sequence provides a better sense of az-Zarkashi's overall approach and the coherence of his framework. The standard Arabic edition is the four-volume set edited by محمد ﷺ Abu al-Fadl إبراهيم ﷺ, the same editor who produced the standard edition of al-Itqan, published in Cairo. This edition has been widely reprinted and is available in both print and digital forms. Other editions, بما في ذلك a more recent البالغ الأهمية edition by Dar al-Turath, are available for الأكاديمي use. The complete text is in major digital Islamic libraries and can be searched and cross-referenced with al-Itqan for comparative study. Students who take the time to compare al-Burhan and al-Itqan on the same topics are rewarded with insight into how Islamic scholarship accumulates and refines itself across generations: they can trace exactly what as-Suyuti added to az-Zarkashi's treatment, where he agreed and where he diverged, and how he reorganized the material for greater accessibility. This comparative exercise is itself an education in the history of Islamic القرآني scholarship that no secondary account can adequately substitute for.
